old_claim.note: Basic Shelter is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No basic-shelter edge remains because it expressed a category relationship between shelter records rather than a demonstrated transfer or technical dependency at Garvald Burn.

source_shape.source_locator: Report pp. 20-22 interprets a dated posthole semicircle beside a hearth and knapping floor as a probable expedient windbreak without identifying an earlier shelter tradition as an input.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The excavated post-and-stakehole feature is interpreted locally from its layout and wind exposure, not from descent from Ohalo II shelters.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Both records belong to shelter technology, but graph edges require causal or historical evidence rather than parent-category membership alone.

ontology_before: A specific windbreak depended on the broad Basic Shelter node solely because both were shelters.
ontology_after: The Garvald Burn feature stands on its own dated archaeological interpretation without a taxonomy-as-causality edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: simple_windbreak_shelters no longer directly depends on basic_shelter.
would_reject_if: Evidence demonstrated a specific technological lineage from the Ohalo II shelter tradition to the Garvald Burn feature.
short_reason: Shared shelter classification is not a demonstrated historical dependency.
